firstView.title: Your Chat Privately Hosted!
firstView.subtitle: VoceChat is a superlight Rust powered chat app, API and SDK that prioritizes private hosting. Build your own in-app messaging feature with VoceChat!
firstView.downloadGooglePlay: download Android APP from Google Play
firstView.downloadApk: download Android APP from APK file
firstView.downloadIos: download iOS APP from app store
firstView.downloadDesktop: download desktop version from github releases

features.heading: Open API and SDK for you to host anywhere. No central service.
features.intro: Bots, video call, publish channel, open ID, open API, open sourced SDK, iterate based on users feedback. Trusted by over <count>40,000</count> customers from 50+ countries.
features.securityItem1: All data is stored on your own, private, server.
features.securityItem2: The server is developed in Rust with critical business process tests with 80%+ coverage.
features.lightweightItem1: The server is less than 20 MB in size and can easily run on a NAS and Raspberry Pi.
features.crossPlatformItem2: Support Android, iOS, MacOS, Web, and Windows platforms.
features.openapiItem1: Utilizes open API standard, with easy integration with third-party applications and tools.

deploy.afterStart: After starting the server, visit http://localhost:3000/ to use the app.

faq.heading: FAQ
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about VoceChat.
faq.a1: Voce Chat is a lightweight, self-hosted team communication platform that provides secure messaging, file sharing, and collaboration features. It's designed to be resource-efficient and easy to deploy, making it ideal for teams who want full control over their communication infrastructure.
faq.q2: How do I install Voce Chat?
faq.a2Lead: Voce Chat can be installed using Docker with a single command:
faq.a2Tail: It also supports installation via binary files or from source for different platforms.
faq.a3: Voce Chat requires only 20MB of RAM and works on Linux, Windows, macOS, and ARM devices like Raspberry Pi. Any VPS with 512MB RAM or more is sufficient for running Voce Chat.
faq.a4: Yes, Voce Chat offers a free self-hosted version with core features including unlimited users, channels, and messages. Paid plans are available for additional enterprise features and support.
faq.a5: Voce Chat provides end-to-end encryption support, SSL/TLS encryption for data in transit, and local data storage under your control. Being self-hosted ensures complete data ownership and privacy.
faq.q6: Does Voce Chat have mobile apps?
faq.a6: Yes, Voce Chat provides native mobile applications for both iOS and Android devices, as well as a responsive web interface that works on any modern browser.
faq.q7: Can I integrate Voce Chat with other tools?
faq.a7: Yes, Voce Chat supports webhooks and API integrations, allowing connections with third-party services and workflow automation. Bot support is also available for custom automation needs.
faq.q8: How does Voce Chat compare to Slack or Discord?
faq.a8: Unlike cloud-based solutions, Voce Chat is self-hosted with complete data ownership, requires minimal resources, has no user limits in the self-hosted version, and offers a simpler interface focused on essential communication features.
faq.q9: Can I migrate from another chat platform?
faq.a9: Voce Chat provides data import tools for migrating from certain platforms. The API also allows for custom migration scripts. Check the documentation for specific migration guides and supported formats.
faq.a10: Help is available through the documentation at <link>doc.voce.chat</link>, community forums, GitHub repository for issue reporting, and direct support for paid plan users.
